slides.title: Your best sleep starts *an hour before bed*.
slides.sub: A calm wind-down routine tells the body the day is over. Here is how it works.

slides.title: The body needs a runway
slides.body: Sleep is a landing, not a switch. A steady wind-down gives your nervous system time to *shift into rest*.

slides.title: Dialing down the alarm
slides.body: Winding down lowers sympathetic arousal, the body's fight-or-flight state. That quiet *can shorten* the time it takes to fall asleep.

slides.title: Let cortisol fall
slides.body: Cortisol should ebb in the evening. A racing, stimulating night *is linked to* a cortisol bump that keeps you wired.

slides.title: Dim, slow, low
slides.body: Soften the lights, set down the screen, and slow your breath. These small signals *can support* an easier drift into sleep.

slides.title: Same time, every night
slides.body: Consistency is the lever. A routine your body learns to expect *may help* it relax on cue, night after night.

slides.title: Make wind-down a *daily habit*.
slides.body: Vibe Health tracks your sleep, food, movement and steps, then turns them into one daily Health Score so your evenings actually pay off.
slides.cta_text: search ‘Vibe Health’ on the App Store

caption:
Falling asleep faster usually starts long before your head hits the pillow. 🌙😌

A calm wind-down lowers stress arousal and lets evening cortisol fall, so sleep comes easier. Dim the lights, slow your breath, keep the timing steady. Save this. 🔖

Vibe Health turns those habits into one daily score → link in bio.
youtube_title: The Wind-Down Routine That Helps You Fall Asleep Faster
